content:
# Session 80 (iter-080) ‚Äî review summary

## Metadata
- **Active-cone sorry: 4 ‚Üí 3.** `TensorObjInverse.lean` 4‚Üí3; PresheafDualPullback.lean 0 (sorry-free since iter-079); SliceTransport.lean 0.
- SOLO `prove` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean`, one file edited.
- Authoritative build (review-run): `lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.TensorObjInverse` ‚Üí **EXIT 0** (8325 jobs), green-mod-sorry, no errors. No new axioms (the file imports the axiom-clean dependency cone; `#print axioms` not name-accessible on `private` decls).
- Targets attempted: (b) `sheafificationCompPullback_restrict` [SOLVED], S3 `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` [PARTIAL], S4a `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat` [partial/blocked], telescope `trivialisation_restrict_compat` [untouched/blocked].

## Target 1 ‚Äî (b) `sheafificationCompPullback_restrict` (~L1116) ‚Äî SOLVED
- **Proof:** `exact sheafificationCompPullback_comp j U.Œπ P` (Sq1 specialised at `h:=j`, `f:=U.Œπ`).
- **Key insight:** the in-file "private blocker" docstring + L1102/L1126/L1172 comments were **STALE** ‚Äî Sq1 `sheafificationCompPullback_comp` (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean`) and its tail helpers are PUBLIC (un-privated iter-069). The goal's RHS is Sq1's RHS verbatim (only cosmetic `(‚Üë¬∑).ringCatSheaf.obj` vs `.val`, defeq, absorbed by `exact`). Unused `_hjŒπ` is legit (LHS uses `j ‚â´ U.Œπ` literally, no rewrite needed). Verified by `lean_multi_attempt` (`goals:[]`, no diagnostics) then `lake build` EXIT 0. Stale BLOCKER docstring/comments deleted on close.
- **Lesson (corollary to iter-068's privacy rule):** a stale "BLOCKER (visibility)" comment is not authoritative ‚Äî re-test `exact <lemma>` before re-deriving; visibility may have been fixed upstream since the comment was written.

## Target 2 ‚Äî S3 `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (~L1185) ‚Äî PARTIAL (the genuine dual-flank hard core)
The opening reduction lands the `case e_Œ≤` residual cleanly:
```
rw [dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison V.Œπ M, dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison U.Œπ M,
    dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison j (M.restrict U.Œπ),
    restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_compat j hjŒπ M.dual]
simp only [Iso.trans_assoc]; congr 1
```
(discharges dual-B1 √ó3 + Bridge-B2 + the `œÅ_{M.dual}` prefix ‚Äî same opening as S2.)

Assembling the residual from bridges (b)+(c) crosses the **`restrictFunctor j` SOURCE-category `SheafOfModules ‚â´` / `U.Modules` instance diamond**. Every structural step fails (`lean_multi_attempt`-confirmed):
- `rw`/`simp [Functor.mapIso_trans]` / `rw [Functor.map_comp]` ‚Üí **"did not find pattern"** (defeq-but-not-syntactic `‚â´`).
- `apply Iso.ext; ‚Ä¶ erw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]` ‚Üí **fires on the WRONG occurrence**, unfolding RFIP's internal `leftUnitor ‚â´ mateEquiv ‚Ä¶` mate composite (catastrophic blow-up).
- `rw [show ‚Ä¶ = ‚Ä¶ from Functor.mapIso_trans _ _ _]` ‚Üí term elaborates (defeq match) but the subsequent `rw` **whnf-bombs** (deterministic timeout at 200000 heartbeats).

**Diagnosis (empirical, not declarative):** S3 must be a **FULL TERM-MODE assembly** (the proven `c2_assemble`/`tensorObj_functoriality_comp3` idiom ‚Äî generic single-`[Category C]` distribution/cancellation lemma applied by `exact`/`refine Eq.trans`, NEVER `rw`/`erw`/`simp` of a category lemma across the seam), with `maxHeartbeats` bumped. It additionally needs **3 dual-coherence helpers that do not yet exist** and must be built first (dual analogues of S2's bricks):
1. `presheafDualPullbackComparison_pullbackCongr` ‚Äî dual of `pullbackTensorMap_pullbackCongr` (L947): Œ∏ across the `pullbackCongr hjŒπ` leg.
2. pre-cancelled `sheafificationCompPullback_restrict_cancel` (`@[reassoc]`) ‚Äî dual of `pullbackTensorMap_restrict_cancel` (L972): folds the leading `pullbackComp` of bridge (b).
3. per-leg dual coherence ‚Äî dual of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_compat_leg` (L990): carries the RFIP_U comparisons past the RFIP_j / SCP_j legs.
With these + (b)+(c) + the term-mode assembly, S3 telescopes exactly as S2 (`tensorObj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat`, L1028) does.

## Target 3 ‚Äî S4a `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat` (~L1221) ‚Äî partial/blocked
Partial proof (`simp only [dual_unit_iso, dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison]`) in place; residual documented in-code (L1210‚Äì1220). Needs the same SCP composition seam as S3's bridge (b) + a Œ∏-unit identification (`presheafDualUnitIso_naturality` promoted to `j`). **lean-auditor `iter080` flags (minor) that the S4a in-code comment overstates the block: bridge (b) is now proven, so S4a is NOT strictly gated on S3 closing and may be partly pursuable in parallel.**

## Target 4 ‚Äî telescope `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (~L1989) ‚Äî untouched/blocked
Strict downstream consumer of S3/S4a; correctly deferred. Wires the 5 trivialisation squares (S2/S4b/S4c done).

## Review subagents
- **lean-auditor** `iter080` (0 critical / 0 major / 3 minor) ‚Äî `task_results/lean-auditor-iter080.md`. (b)-close GENUINE (real `exact`, not a vacuous/mismatched close; `.val` vs `.obj` resolved by defeq). Three minor comment-hygiene flags: (i) L1159 "sorry-free iter-079" should read iter-080 (prover off-by-one ‚Äî recurs across iters); (ii) L1155 "the iter-068 BLOCKER notes below are STALE" ‚Äî those notes no longer exist, "below" is a dead pointer; (iii) L1219 S4a comment "Both are downstream of the S3 residual" overstates the block. No excuse-comments, no weakened defs, no unauthorized axioms, all 3 sorries genuine residuals.
- **lean-vs-blueprint-checker** `tensorobjinverse-iter080` (**0 must-fix** / 2 major / 2 minor) ‚Äî `task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-tensorobjinverse-iter080.md`. Lean faithfully tracks the blueprint; the 3 sorries are honest marker-consistent WIP (statement `\leanok` present = decl exists; proof `\leanok` absent = proof open ‚Äî consistent, not deceptive). Major #1: bridge-(b) `\uses` over-credits 3 bypassed lemmas (realized proof is the one-liner) ‚Üí trim. Major #2 (non-blocking, lvb declined "must-fix inadequacy" framing ‚Äî S2 formalized sorry-free from structurally-identical prose): S3 proof block lacks an implementation note for the term-mode obstacle + the 3 dual helper bricks. Both ‚Üí blueprint-writer next iter.

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict` (L7860): added `% NOTE` (lvb major #1) ‚Äî `\uses` over-credits 3 bypassed lemmas; realized proof's sole real dep is `lem:sheafificationcomppullback_comp`; flagged for blueprint-writer to trim.
- `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` `lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` proof block (L8425): added `% NOTE` (lvb major #2) ‚Äî bridges (b),(c) proven but the residual needs term-mode assembly + 3 NEW dual-coherence helper blocks; flagged for blueprint-writer to add an impl-note + the 3 helper blocks.
- No `\leanok` override needed: `sync_leanok` ran for iter-080 (sha 032abf1, +0/‚àí0); lvb confirmed markers consistent with the 3 open sorries.

## Blueprint doctor
- Clean: no orphan chapters, no broken `\ref`/`\uses`/`\proves`, no new `axiom` declarations.

## Key findings / patterns
- **Stale "visibility BLOCKER" comments mask one-line closes.** (b) had been blocked-by-comment for ~12 iters (iter-068 onward); the underlying privacy was already fixed iter-069. Re-test the intended `exact` before believing a stale blocker note.
- **The `restrictFunctor j` source-category instance diamond defeats tactic-mode category rewrites** ‚Äî same class of seam already documented for the `SheafOfModules ‚â´` over-`Z` seam (iters 063/074). Cure is the same: lift the whole chase to a generic single-`[Category C]` lemma and `exact`/`refine` it.

## Recommendations for next session
See `recommendations.md`. Headline: build the 3 S3 dual-coherence helpers (scaffold/prove) FIRST, then term-mode-assemble S3; S4a is partly parallel-able; blueprint-writer handles the 2 lvb majors.
